quality.rationale: 利用四维速度内积的洛伦兹不变性结合三维矢量代数进行多步推导，逻辑严密且步骤完整

origin.question:
Problem 1.3. If two frames move with 3-velocities $\underline{\mathbf{v}}_1$ and $\underline{\mathbf{v}}_2$ , show that their relative velocity is given by
$$
v^{2} = \frac {\left(v_{1} - v_{2}\right)^{2} - \left(v_{1} \times v_{2}\right)^{2}}{\left(1 - v_{1} \cdot v_{2}\right)^{2}}.
$$
origin.solution:
Let $\mathbf{u}_1$ and $\mathbf{u}_2$ be the 4-velocities of the two frames. In frame 1, $\mathbf{u}_1 = (1,0)$ , $\mathbf{u}_2 = (\gamma, \gamma \mathbf{v})$ where $\gamma = (1 - v^2)^{- rac{1}{2}}$ . Since $\gamma = -\mathbf{u}_1 \cdot \mathbf{u}_2$ ,
we just have to evaluate this scalar in a general frame where $\mathbf{u}_1 = (\gamma_1, \gamma_1 \underline{\mathbf{v}}_1)$ , $\mathbf{u}_2 = (\gamma_2, \gamma_2 \underline{\mathbf{v}}_2)$ :
$$
\gamma = - \mathbf {u}_{1} \cdot \mathbf {u}_{2} = \gamma_{1} \gamma_{2} - \gamma_{1} \gamma_{2} \underset {\sim} {\mathrm {v}}_{1} \cdot \underset {\sim} {\mathrm {v}}_{2} = (1 - \mathrm {v}^{2})^{-  rac {1}{2}}
$$
$$
1 - v^{2} = \left(y_{1} y_{2}\right)^{- 2} / \left(1 - \underline {{v}}_{1} \cdot \underline {{v}}_{2}\right)^{2}
$$
$$
v^{2} = \frac {\left(1 - \underline {{v}}_{1} \cdot \underline {{v}}_{2}\right)^{2} - \left(1 - v_{1}^{2}\right) \left(1 - v_{2}^{2}\right)}{\left(1 - \underline {{v}}_{1} \cdot \underline {{v}}_{2}\right)^{2}} = \frac {\left(\underline {{v}}_{1} - \underline {{v}}_{2}\right)^{2} - \left(\underline {{v}}_{1} \times \underline {{v}}_{2}\right)^{2}}{\left(1 - \underline {{v}}_{1} \cdot \underline {{v}}_{2}\right)^{2}}.
$$
(The solution could also be verified by a painfully tedious application of Lorentz transformations.)
